description: Shipping a container whose process runs as root because no non-root USER was set, widening the blast radius of any escape.
body: Failure mode: the Dockerfile never adds a USER directive, so the application runs as root inside the container; a vulnerability or escape then has root in the container and an easier path to the host. Detection signals: the running container's process is UID 0; no USER instruction in the Dockerfile; volume files are created root-owned. Do not run application code as root for convenience. Safer fallback: create and switch to a non-root user, ensure needed paths are owned/writable by it, and verify the process runs unprivileged. Risk boundary: for workloads needing root capabilities, scope them explicitly and escalate rather than running fully privileged by default.
